titleStmt.title: SII 29.12: original edition by P. N. Narasimha Murthy
No. 12. (A.R. No. 71 of 1961-62.) KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the same pillar, Museum No. 88.

It records that during the reign of Chālukya Jagadēkamalla manevarggaḍe Nannapayya, the possessor of traya-guṇa made a gift of the village Gāḍicheṟuvu situated within Ānemārga-70 adjoining Koḷḷipāke-7000 for the worship and offerings to the god Nanni-Nārayaṇa. Refers to Surēndradēva, probably the sthānapati of the place. The said gift was made on the occasion of Solar eclipse.
It is dated Śaka 954, Āngīrasa, Āshāḍha, Amāvāsye, Monday. It corresponds to 1032 A.D., July 10, Monday. There was Solar eclipse on that day.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti samasta bhuvanāśra
ya śrī pṛthvīvallabha ma
hārājādhirāja pa
ramēśvara paramabhaṭṭāra
kaṁ satyāśraya ku
ḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābhara
ṇaṁ śrīmajjagadēka ma
lladēvara vijayarā
jyamuttarōttarābhivṛddhi
pravardhamāna mācaṁdrā
rkka tāraṁbaraṁ saluttami
re tatpāda padmōpa jīvi
samasta rājyabharanirūpi
ta mahāmātya padavī
rājamāna mānōnnatapra
bhumaṁtrōtsāha śaktitra
ya guṇa saṁpannarappa śrī
manmane verggaḍe nannapayyaṁ
gaḷu koḷḷipākeya
śrī surēndradēva
si (vayu ci)
(padiṁ
śrī suNote-(1) Lines 23-27 are engraved
rēndradē
vari sthā
na mapra
tipāḷisidar .Note-(1) Lines 23-27 are engraved on the top of the record to the right side of the

śrī nanni nārāyaṇadēva
rgge śrījagadēkama
lla dēvar sakavarṣa
954 neya Aṁgīrasa saṁvatsa
radāṣaḍhadamāvāsya sōma
vāradandu sūryyagrahaṇa
nimittadiṁ koḷḷipāke Ercchāsi
rada baḷiya Ānemārgame
ppattaṟoḷagaṇa gāḍiceṟu
vulaṁ sarvanamasyamāge
koṭṭar . © © sāmā
nyōyaṁ dharmmasētu nṛpāṇāṁ
kālē kālē pālaniyō bha
vadbhiḥ sarvvānētānbhāvinarṛpā
rtthivēndrān bhūyō bhū
yō yācatē rāmabhadraḥ .
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vāyō
harēti vasundarā ṣaṣṭi
rvvarṣa sahaśrāṇi viṣṭā
yāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ .
bahubhirvvasudhā dattā rāja
bhissagarā(dhi)bhiḥ yasya
yasya yadābhūmiḥ tasya
tasya tadā phaḷaṁ . ©
